title: California Wonâ€™t Replace Expiring $7,500 Federal EV Tax Credit
content:
California will not backfill a $7,500 federal tax credit for buyers of electric vehicles that is set to expire this month, Governor Gavin Newsom said on Friday, reversing an earlier pledge to restart the stateâ€™s own EV subsidies.

â€œWe canâ€™t make up for federal vandalism of those tax credits,â€ Newsom said during a press conference in San Francisco. The state will support expanding EV infrastructure, but â€œnot the direct subsidies that we cannot make up for,â€ the Democrat said.

President Donald Trump campaigned on rolling back many of former President Joe Bidenâ€™s EV-friendly policies. Congress followed through in the major tax-and-spending bill, which placed a Sept. 30 expiration on the tax credit, ending years of federal support for EV buyers.

Newsomâ€™s declaration all but dashes near-term hope among EV makers such as Rivian Automotive Inc., Hyundai Motor Co. and Volkswagen AG that the countryâ€™s largest auto market would fill that void. Those automakers and others had urged the governor and state leaders to create a $5,000 EV incentive.
author: Analysts Account
